California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
August 8, 1991
Kinberly Schuld
Legislative Aide
state capitol
P. O. Box 942449
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001
Re: Your Request For Informal Advice
Our Fil-e No. l-9L-322
Dear Ms. Schu1d:
You have requested advice on behaLf of Assemblyman PauI
Horcher concerning the gift provi.sions of the Polj-tical Reform Act
(the "Act").r 
Because of the general nature of yogr inquiry, your
request is treated as one for informal assistance.z
OUESTION
If Assenblyman Horcher pays only 5750 of a 51,o00 airport
parking bi1l, and the balance is forgiven, what is the value of
any gift to hin fron the airport?
CONCLUS TON
If Assenblyman Horcher pays only S750 of a S1.000 parking
bill, 
he receives a gift of S25O fron the airport.
1 co.r".rr."nt code sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 caLifornia Code of Regulations Section
18OOO, et seo. AII references to reguJ.ations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the california code of Requlations.
In an earlier advice letter to you, Schuld Advice Letter, A-
gL-O74, we discussed Assenbllanan Horcher's use of campaign funds
to pay for a parking pass.
2 Infonnal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
imnunity provided by an opinion or fornal written advice.
(Section 84113,' Regulation 18329(c) (3) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 122-5660


Our Fi■e No.  
■-9■-322
Page 2
FACTS
AssembLyman Horcher has a VIp parking pass for parking at
Ontario Airport. 
The airport annually bil1s the assemblymin based
upon the number of days the pass is used. You have asked us to
assume that Assernbl-)rman Horcher Day receive a bilL of 51,ooo for
use of the parking pass. (we assume that the 91,ooo is the fair
market value of the parking privileges received by Assenbl)rman
Horcher. ) The airport is wiJ.Iing to waive aII or part of the
bill. 
However, Assenbl)rman Horcher does not lrish to run afoul of
any gift liroitations. 
He is considering paying 9750 of the 51,OOO
birl.
ANALYSIS
Under Section 82028(a), ,',Gift, neans . . . any palrment to
the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not
received 
rr If Assemblyman Horcher pays the airport S75O
for the S1,000 bill, 
the airport wiIl have received consideration
of $750. The gift wouJ.d only be the anount not covered by the
consideration, or S250. Please note that under recently enacted
ethics laws, Assemblyman Horcher is Iimited to 5250 in gifts from
any sj-ngle source per calendar year. Thus if he were to receive a
gift of 5250 from the airport, he s/ould be precluded from
receiving another gift from the airport for the rema j.nder of the
calendar year.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter
please contact me at (916) 322-59OL.3
Sincerely.
Scott HaI labrin
Acting ceneral counsel

cost of 10C per page.
